specific_location: 5 miles south of Lamar on Highway 287 then a little over a quarter of a mile to the west. The bird was at the water feature in the back yard of the farm house.
description: The size of the bird was a little larger than a Lark Sparrow, noticeably smaller than a Yellow-headed Blackbird, and about the same size as a Lark Bunting. I mention these three species because all three were present at the water.   When the bird first flew through the yard away from us, we noticed the striking black wings and white rump and first thought of a woodpecker. However when the bird appeared again at the water, the obvious yellow nape made the identification unmistakably a Bobolink. The entire front of the head and underneath was all black and bold white markings were at the base (or scapulars) of each wing.Behaviors: The only behavior we observed was that the bird was flying into the yard obviously for a drink of water.Call: I did not distinguish the call but my fellow observer did hearPlumage: Adult breeding male
photographer: Photographer: Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp, Jane Stulp
